findings.fix: Add '- [project.md](.claude/skills/project-knowledge/project.md)' to the Project knowledge block.

findings.issue: Mandatory context file 'project.md' is absent from Context Files. The Project knowledge block lists 'overview.md', 'architecture.md', 'deployment.md' but omits 'project.md' which is required for all tasks.
findings.fix: Add '- [project.md](.claude/skills/project-knowledge/project.md)' to the Project knowledge block.

findings.issue: TDD Anchor section is present but contains no test entries in the required format ('`tests/path::test_name` — description'). The section body explains that automated unit tests are not feasible for the top/ package and lists hypothetical future test cases in plain prose. For a code-writing task the TDD Anchor must either contain properly-formatted test entries or be deleted entirely. A prose rationale for why tests cannot be written is not a valid TDD Anchor.
findings.fix: If tests genuinely cannot be written for this task (top/ package requires a live gocui terminal), delete the TDD Anchor section entirely. The absence of the section is the correct signal for an untestable code path. Alternatively, if any helper pure functions are introduced (e.g., a function that computes the guard condition without calling gocui), write a test for those and list them here in the required format.

findings.issue: Task modifies 4 files (top/keybindings.go, top/config_view.go, top/dialog.go, top/help.go), which exceeds the 3-file atomicity guideline. The changes are logically cohesive (all wire one new screen into the TUI interaction layer), and the tech-spec explicitly groups them as a single task. Flagged for awareness; not a blocker.
findings.fix: No action required if the reviewer agrees the changes represent a single logical unit. If preferred, top/dialog.go (guard restructuring) could be split into a separate sub-task, but this is at reviewer discretion.

findings.issue: The Details section does not have an explicit 'Files' subsection with file paths and their current states. The section opens with 'No code changes in this task.' and then has separate paragraphs for dependencies, test infrastructure, edge cases, etc., but uses prose headers (bold text) rather than the structured subsections expected by the template ('Files', 'Dependencies', 'Edge cases', 'Implementation hints'). For a non-code task 'Files' may be omitted, but 'Implementation hints' subsection is absent.
findings.fix: Add an explicit '**Implementation hints:**' subsection summarising the practical guidance already present in the Details prose (e.g., what to do when make test fails, how to interpret race output, etc.).
